questions.q: Which of the following movements was primarily concerned with the abolition of the salt tax?
Explain the significance of the Non-Cooperation Movement launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920.
questions.answer: The movement marked a shift from non-violent resistance to mass-based non-cooperation with the British colonial rule.

questions.q: Describe the role of the Khilafat Movement in the Indian freedom struggle during the 1920s.
questions.answer: The Khilafat Movement was a significant development in the Indian freedom struggle as it brought together Hindus and Muslims to fight against British colonial rule. The movement, led by Mahatma Gandhi and Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar, aimed to protect the Ottoman Caliphate and the rights of Indian Muslims.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The _______________________ Movement was launched in 1929 to achieve complete independence from British rule.
True or False: The Non-Cooperation Movement was a complete success in achieving its objectives.
Describe the impact of the Salt March Movement on the Indian freedom struggle.
questions.answer: The Salt March Movement, led by Mahatma Gandhi, had a significant impact on the Indian freedom struggle. It galvanized public opinion against British colonial rule and marked a shift towards non-violent resistance.

questions.q: Which of the following was a major objective of the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.answer: To boycott British goods and institutions

questions.q: Explain the significance of the Khilafat Movement in the Indian freedom struggle.
questions.answer: The Khilafat Movement marked a significant development in the Indian freedom struggle as it brought together Hindus and Muslims to fight against British colonial rule.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The _______________________ Civil Disobedience Movement was launched in 1930 to protest the Salt Act.
True or False: The Salt March Movement was a spontaneous response to British colonial rule.
